findings.finding: The live programme records a known red Fabric and Rust integration baseline, so current main is not a safe basis for write-profile activation or stable release.
evidence: current-head connector query returned no PR workflow evidence
findings.recommendation: Create a machine-visible integration-red state, complete Lane D and Rust reconciliation, then require current-head clean evidence before write pilots.
findings.acceptance: One current main commit passes the complete root, Fabric, Console, Herdr and Rust matrix; the result is visible in status/Console and protected by required checks.

findings.finding: Specs 01 and 04 are approximately 9,733 and 8,458 lines, contain known P0/P1 defects and have explicitly incomplete audit coverage.
findings.recommendation: Repair, split and freeze the spec families before AuthorityEnvelopeV2 depends on them. Remove amendment history from current normative bodies.
findings.acceptance: Every current spec module is <=1,000 lines, has a single subject owner, passes requirement-ID uniqueness/traceability checks and has no unresolved P0-P1 audit finding.

findings.finding: Global policy mandates human spec/final-acceptance gates while the programme charter delegates those gates to an LLM chair/council; the protocol and delivery receipt still encode human resolution.
findings.recommendation: Generalise the charter into a typed, human-approved decision-delegation contract consumed by the delivery validator, Fabric and Console.
findings.acceptance: For every decision class, all surfaces agree on chair/council/human authority; a delegated decision has an authenticated resolution type rather than masquerading as human evidence.

findings.finding: Managed primary-provider execution remains read-only; the capability-profile work has not reached AuthorityEnvelopeV2, AuthorityCompiler or a containment-proven write pilot.
findings.recommendation: Do not shortcut the staged plan. Complete spec freeze, V2 cutover, pure compiler extraction and adversarial containment before one-provider workspace-write-offline.
findings.acceptance: A provider can write only in one owned worktree with no egress/effects, and hostile path, Git, symlink, settings, secret, lifecycle and process tests pass.

findings.finding: Operational authority is duplicated across constitution, architecture, specs, ADRs, charter, decision register, effort map, handoffs and the old review pack.
findings.recommendation: Adopt a document-routing policy with a canonical type/claim key, migrate live work to specs/ADRs/issues/project policy, and retire working-pack authority.
findings.acceptance: Every current claim resolves to exactly one canonical owner; generated indexes detect duplicates; superseded working documents contain no live directives.

findings.finding: Issue forms exist, but the active implementation graph is not issue-backed and the accepted backlog-item schema remains unimplemented.
findings.recommendation: Publish the vertical-slice work-item schema and select GitHub Issues as Provenant's canonical work store; use docs/issues only for non-GitHub projects.
findings.acceptance: Every active implementation slice has one issue with spec/ADR links, dependencies, authority, write scope and acceptance; effort maps no longer own task status.

findings.finding: Current scope/implement rules return any scope or design drift to a fresh human gate, contrary to the desired delegated, low-handholding implementation mode.
findings.recommendation: Introduce three delta classes: implementation detail, delegated reversible spec delta, and hard authority/one-way-door change.
findings.acceptance: Class A/B deltas proceed under the delegation charter, update issue/spec/PR and raise a soft decision; only Class C blocks for human authority.

findings.finding: Fabric models blocking scoped gates but lacks a first-class non-blocking decision request with an applied default and human override window.
findings.recommendation: Add informational, soft and hard decision modes; keep hard decisions as gates and project soft decisions into Attention without blocking work.
findings.acceptance: A soft decision shows default, rationale, council record, override cut-point and related issue/PR; work proceeds and any override becomes a traceable replan.

findings.finding: The charter uses 'council vote' language even though the constitution says objective evidence outranks opinions and decisions must not be majority-voted into truth.
findings.recommendation: Rename the mechanism council deliberation; require independent first passes and chair adjudication against evidence, policy and oracle strength.
findings.acceptance: Council records contain claims, evidence, dissent and adjudication; vote count is never sufficient to override deterministic evidence or authority.

findings.finding: The programme charter mandates one monolithic PR, while its per-leg loop says to open a PR after each leg; neither is a suitable global default.
findings.recommendation: Derive PR topology from task dependencies and path conflicts: independent, stacked, consolidated, or no-PR/direct-commit by project policy.
findings.acceptance: Each plan records a conflict graph and PR strategy; concurrently open PRs are mergeable in the declared order and do not race on canonical docs/generated files.

findings.finding: The branch-protection ruleset is only a proposal and deliberately not applied; direct commits appear after the PR-based foundation change.
findings.recommendation: After the red baseline is repaired, apply branch rules consistent with the selected project policy and test the ruleset as repository assurance.
findings.acceptance: Current main can advance only through the authorised route, and the route is consistent with the project delegation/PR policy.

findings.finding: Fabric remains 7,403 lines and a new lifecycle engine is approximately 4,427 lines, creating multiple high-context application centres.
findings.recommendation: Continue vertical extraction through existing seams, preceded by architecture/import tests; alert on responsibility/change-pressure rather than only line count.
findings.acceptance: Provider admission, lifecycle custody and projection responsibilities have focused owners, forbidden imports are gated and old paths are deleted.

findings.finding: Console presenter and renderer/interaction entrypoint are each approximately 1,900 lines.
findings.recommendation: Separate view models, text safety, layout, rendering, hit regions and input reduction; keep protocol bindings immutable.
findings.acceptance: Individual views and reducers are independently testable; no principal Console module mixes Unicode handling, full layout and action interaction.

findings.finding: The dependency-free Rust supervisor is approximately 2,549 lines and explicitly does not yet prove the certifying-review containment contract.
findings.recommendation: Keep capability=false until daemon integration and escape canaries pass; split process, framing and custody modules where ownership is stable.
findings.acceptance: Both hosted platforms pass, outer confinement is attested and every required failure/death path is tested end to end.

findings.finding: Tracked adapter compatibility still mixes portable policy with absolute workstation executable paths and local digests.
findings.recommendation: Keep portable adapter contracts/ranges in Git and generate private local attestation overlays through doctor/activation.
findings.acceptance: A clean checkout contains no user-machine executable path; local pins remain exact and fail closed through a private overlay.

findings.finding: Spec 01 says the specification owns its decisions with no separate ADR, while accepted ADRs now own major architecture decisions.
findings.recommendation: Define precedence: ADR owns decision/rationale, current spec owns normative behaviour, Git owns history.
findings.acceptance: Specs link decisions but do not claim competing decision ownership; ADR changes trigger exact spec updates.

findings.finding: The chair charter's embedded lane snapshot says Lane B is not started, while the current effort map says it is complete and merged.
findings.recommendation: Remove mutable status snapshots from policy/charter documents; project live state only from issues/Fabric.
findings.acceptance: No durable policy document embeds mutable lane status; stale-state checks flag any such section.

findings.finding: Move-never-delete defaults preserve working history in the live documentation tree and increase agent context and drift risk.
findings.recommendation: Use Git history as the default archive; retain/move only audit, legal or operational evidence required by policy. Delete superseded working docs after reference migration.
findings.acceptance: A prune action can delete known superseded docs under policy, refuses unknown files, and leaves one current owner plus immutable Git history.

findings.finding: The accepted <=1,000-line spec-family gate is not present in the current harness gate, and docs/references are explicitly outside the style gate.
findings.recommendation: Add document schemas, spec size/name/ownership checks, link/duplicate-key checks and changed-file style gating.
findings.acceptance: CI rejects numbered new spec names, >1,000-line spec modules, duplicate canonical keys, stale indexes and unowned active working docs.

findings.finding: Risk/oracle-adjusted review is accepted but the binding constitution and Spec 05 still mandate broader fixed review profiles.
findings.recommendation: Land one coherent amendment and validator change before relying on the new policy.
findings.acceptance: Risk/oracle inputs deterministically derive required reviewers, and no current document or validator retains the superseded profile.

findings.finding: Retention classes are accepted but class tagging and governed deletion remain unimplemented.
findings.recommendation: Class-tag new docs/run artefacts now; implement deletion after the write-profile prerequisites without extending archive-only as a permanent policy.
findings.acceptance: Every retained artefact has a class/owner/expiry; deletion preview/apply is protected, hold-aware and receipt-bearing.

findings.finding: The desired autonomous creation/update of GitHub issues is an external collaboration effect but no standing project authority policy is defined for it.
findings.recommendation: Register issue create/update/close as bounded external operations and allow a project charter to pre-authorise them independently of release/push/merge.
findings.acceptance: Agents can raise traceable issues without human micro-approval, but cannot broaden labels, repositories, recipients or other external effects beyond the charter.

findings.finding: AGENTS still requires full HARNESS loading for orchestration and retains `$caveman` as the global style default despite a later accepted style split.
findings.recommendation: Update the bootstrap to load document/decision policy progressively and use domain-appropriate human-facing prose.
findings.acceptance: Global instructions and ADR agree; routine tasks do not load unrelated deep governance.

findings.finding: Architecture-review promotion is accepted but the skill is not in the 33-skill catalogue.
findings.recommendation: Implement it through the normal skill-authoring/evaluation path after the document policy is in place.
findings.acceptance: The skill has a distinct trigger, read-only authority, outputs, nearest-neighbour tests and generated catalogue entry.

findings.finding: The PR template is evidence-rich but still assumes historical format readability and human gates in ways that may conflict with direct cutover and delegated decisions.
findings.recommendation: Add direct-cutover/not-applicable paths, scope-delta and soft-decision sections, issue closure and conflict/stack strategy.
findings.acceptance: PRs describe current policy without implying compatibility or human gates that do not apply.

findings.finding: The original review pack remains a live execution route even though its proposals were dispositioned and the repo has been renamed.
findings.recommendation: Extract current decisions into ADR/spec/policy/issues; retain the pack only as frozen evidence or delete it from the live tree after the programme closes.
findings.acceptance: Fresh agents never need the old review pack to determine current authority or work state.
